fresh_facts.claim: Pentagon is preparing to send 3,000 additional troops to the Middle East, building on 50,000 already in the region, amid US airstrikes on Iran since February 28 that have destroyed over 9,000 targets including missile launchers and navy assets.
3,000-4,000 soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division are deploying as an emergency response force to the Middle East with no decision on ground entry into Iran.
Approximately 2,500 Marines are deploying via warships including USS Boxer, USS Portland, and USS Comstock under Operation Epic Fury.
The US sent Iran a 15-point ceasefire proposal as an 'off-ramp' while considering military force to reopen the Strait of Hormuz blocked by Iran.
President Trump extended the deadline for Iran to reach a deal with the US to March 27, 2026.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed supreme leader by the Assembly of Experts after Ali Khamenei's death on March 5, 2026.
Ali Larijani was killed on March 17, 2026, during the ongoing US-Israeli military campaign.
IRGC hardliners reportedly dominate decision-making as strikes prevent Assembly of Experts meetings as of March 2-6, 2026.
Regime personnel losses on March 21 include Basij deputy Azim Esmaeili, Khamenei's missile representative Reza Ebrahimi, and IRGC commander Qader Azarian.
Over 20 countries condemned Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure on March 21, 2026, pledging safe passage support.
US-Israeli strikes have killed over 1,300 people in Iran since February 28, 2026, with 13 US and 15 Israeli deaths reported.
US-Israeli strikes targeted Natanz nuclear site and were conducted repeatedly on March 2 and March 21, 2026.
A jailbreak was reported in Mariwan on March 2, 2026, following US-Israeli strikes on internal security sites.
Cumulative protests persist into 2026 met with regime repression, with chants targeting both reformer and hardliner factions.
Reformist President Masoud Pezeshkian's government has failed to pacify unrest despite acknowledging economic grievances.
Iran blocked the Strait of Hormuz, causing global oil disruptions, which Trump delayed strikes on Iranian power plants to address through peace talks.
Iran denied direct involvement in attacks against the US, despite ongoing Iranian drone and missile attacks on Israel as of March 24, 2026.
CENTCOM confirmed declining Iranian military capabilities via strike documentation and video evidence on March 21, 2026.
Trump considered winding down military operations as Iran-Israel traded attacks as of March 21, 2026.
ACLED analysis notes no evidence of internal fractures within Iranian regime institutions despite Khamenei's death and ongoing strikes as of March 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: The Iranian regime faces sustained military degradation with over 9,000 targets destroyed and strikes across 20+ cities including critical nuclear and missile facilities, while navigating a succession crisis with newly appointed Mojtaba Khamenei amid explicit US rejection and a March 27 ceasefire deadline. A 15-point US ceasefire proposal offers diplomatic off-ramp, but Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade and continued drone/missile attacks indicate regime rejection of current terms, alongside declining military capabilities and personnel losses among senior commanders.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Pentagon announced deployment of 3,000-4,000 additional troops (including 2,500 Marines via USS Boxer, USS Portland, and USS Comstock under Operation Epic Fury, plus 82nd Airborne Division forces) to the Middle East on March 24, while White House presented a 15-point ceasefire proposal and extended negotiation deadline to March 27, 2026.
